questions.question: What is the maximum number of characters allowed in a Unix file name?
options: D) Unrestricted
questions.explanation: The maximum number of characters allowed in a Unix file name is 256.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the '.' character at the beginning of a Unix file name?
options: A) To indicate a hidden file
B) To indicate a directory
C) To indicate a file with a special permission
D) To indicate a file with a specific extension
questions.explanation: The '.' character at the beginning of a Unix file name indicates a hidden file.

questions.question: What is the difference between a Unix file name and a Unix directory name?
options: A) A file name cannot be an empty string while a directory name can
B) A file name cannot contain the '/' character while a directory name can
C) A file name is case sensitive while a directory name is not
D) A file name is always case insensitive while a directory name is always case sensitive
questions.explanation: A Unix file name and a Unix directory name are case sensitive.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the '_' character in a Unix file name?
options: A) To indicate a file with a special permission
B) To indicate a file that is not hidden
C) To indicate a directory
D) To be used as a wildcard character
questions.explanation: The '_' character in a Unix file name can be used as a wildcard character.

questions.question: Can a Unix file name contain spaces?
options: A) Yes
B) No
C) It depends on the context
D) Only in some situations
questions.explanation: A Unix file name cannot contain spaces.
